response:
1. The author is dealt two cards from a standard 52-card deck. To calculate the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards, we use the complement rule. The total number of ways to choose 5 cards from 52 is C(52, 5). The number of ways to choose 5 cards without any hearts is C(36, 5). The probability of no hearts is C(36, 5)/C(52, 5). The probability of at least one heart is 1 - [C(36, 5)/C(52, 5)]. This is the closed form of the probability.  

***  

2. Strategy A has an expected value of 0.4 * 1000 - 0.6 * 500 = 400 - 300 = 100. Strategy B has an expected value of 0.6 * 800 - 0.4 * 200 = 480 - 80 = 400. The author should choose Strategy B as it has a higher expected value.
